description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: 1990–1991 conflict between Iraq and a 42-country coalition
description_wiki: The Gulf War was an armed conflict between Iraq and a 42-country coalition led by the United States.

description: president of the United States from 1989 to 1993 (1924–2018)
description_wiki: George Herbert Walker Bush (June 12, 1924 – November 30, 2018) was the 41st president of the United States, serving from 1989 to 1993.

description: American general and diplomat (1937–2021)
description_wiki: Colin Luther Powell ( KOH-lin POW-əl; (1937-04-05)April 5, 1937 – (2021-10-18)October 18, 2021) was an American Army general, diplomat, and statesman who was the 65th United States secretary of state from 2001 to 2005, being the first Black American to hold the office, and was the highest-ranking Black American in the federal executive branch in American history (along with his successor Condoleezza Rice) until the election of Barack Obama as president in 2008.

description: United States Army general (1934–2012)
description_wiki: Herbert Norman Schwarzkopf Jr.

aliases: Air National Guard of the United States
description: air warfare component of the United States National Guard
description_wiki: The Air National Guard (ANG), also known as the Air Guard, is a federal military reserve force of the United States Air Force (USAF), as well as the "air militia" of each U.S. state, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and the territories of Guam and the U.S. Virgin Islands.

surface_forms: McIntyre International Guard Base is outside of Columbia
description: airport
description_wiki: McEntire Joint National Guard Base or McEntire JNGB (IATA: MMT, ICAO: KMMT, FAA LID: MMT) is a military airport located in Richland County, South Carolina, United States, 10 miles (16 km) west of the town of Eastover and approximately 15 miles southeast of the city of Columbia.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: president of the United States from 1981 to 1989 and actor (1911–2004)
description_wiki: Ronald Wilson Reagan (February 6, 1911 – June 5, 2004) was an American politician and actor who served as the 40th president of the United States from 1981 to 1989.

description: country in Eastern Europe and Northern Asia
description_wiki: Russia, or the Russian Federation, is a country in Eastern Europe and North Asia.

description: sovereign state in Western Asia
description_wiki: Iraq, officially the Republic of Iraq, is a country in West Asia.

description: illlnesses affecting Gulf War Vets
description_wiki: Gulf War syndrome (GWS) also known as Gulf War illness or chronic multi-symptom Illness, is a chronic and multi-symptomatic disorder affecting military veterans of the Gulf War (1990–1991).

description: capital of South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Columbia is the capital city of the U.S. state of South Carolina.

description: city in and county seat of Sumter County, South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Sumter ( SUM-tər) is a city in and the county seat of Sumter County, South Carolina, United States.

description: capital and largest city of Saudi Arabia
description_wiki: Riyadh is the capital and largest city of Saudi Arabia.

description: country in West Asia
description_wiki: Saudi Arabia, officially the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (KSA), is a country in West Asia.

description: tactical ballistic missile family
description_wiki: A Scud missile is one of a series of tactical ballistic missiles developed by the Soviet Union during the Cold War.

description: American multinational ice cream parlor chain
description_wiki: Baskin-Robbins, Inc.

description: American restaurant chain and international franchise
description_wiki: Pizza Hut, LLC is an American multinational pizza restaurant chain and international franchise founded in 1958 in Wichita, Kansas, by brothers Dan and Frank Carney.

description: 1974 multi-role combat aircraft family developed by General Dynamics and built by several manufacturers
description_wiki: The General Dynamics (now Lockheed Martin) F-16 Fighting Falcon is an American single-engine supersonic multirole fourth-generation fighter aircraft developed by General Dynamics and produced by multiple companies, including General Dynamics until 1993 and Lockheed Martin until 2017.

description: Iraqi politician and revolutionary, president of Iraq from 1979 to 2003
description_wiki: Saddam Hussein (28 April 1937 – 30 December 2006) was an Iraqi politician and revolutionary who served as the fifth president of Iraq from 1979 until his overthrow in 2003 during the United States-led invasion of Iraq.

description: sovereign state in Western Asia
description_wiki: Kuwait, officially the State of Kuwait, is a country in West Asia.

description: geopolitical region encompassing Egypt and most of Western Asia, including Iran
description_wiki: The Middle East is a geopolitical region encompassing the Arabian Peninsula, Egypt, Iran, Iraq, the Levant, and Turkey.

description: holiday celebrated in the United States on the fourth Thursday in November
description_wiki: Thanksgiving is a federal holiday in the United States celebrated on the fourth Thursday of November (which became the uniform date country-wide in 1941).

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Connecticut (  kə-NET-ih-kət) is a state in the New England region of the Northeastern United States.

description: airbase in Delaware, USA
description_wiki: Dover Air Force Base or Dover AFB (IATA: DOV, ICAO: KDOV, FAA LID: DOV) is a United States Air Force (USAF) base under the operational control of Air Mobility Command (AMC), located 2 mi (3.2 km) southeast of the city of Dover, Delaware.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: US Air Force base in Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany
description_wiki: Ramstein Air Base (IATA: RMS, ICAO: ETAR) (before 1958 Ramstein–Landstuhl) is a United States Air Force installation located in Rhineland-Palatinate, southwestern Germany.

description: city in Saudi Arabia
description_wiki: Dhahran (Arabic: الظهران‎, romanized: aẓ-Ẓahrān, standard pronunciation: [aðˤ.ðˤah.raːn]) is a city located in the Eastern Province, Saudi Arabia.

description: extension of the Indian Ocean
description_wiki: The Persian Gulf, sometimes called the Arabian Gulf, is a mediterranean sea in West Asia.

description: military unit
description_wiki: The New York Air National Guard (NY ANG) is the aerial militia of the U.S. state of New York.
